stemHtml: During the cruise, modern large jet aeroplanes normally fly at speeds faster than M<sub>CRIT</sub> since the increase in speed is more significant than the increase in drag. This is achieved:

options.b: By decreasing the leading-edge radius of the wing.
options.c: By flying at lower altitudes where the TAS is higher for a given Mach number and therefore experience a delay in the onset of shock waves and wave drag.
options.d: By using an advanced wing technology with a greater curvature underneath to balance the acceleration over the top and therefore reduce the severity of the shock waves.
